item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.4 No.23-24, 1956
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI's CHRONICLE No. 23-24 (83-84) Vol. IV 1956
...nothing affirmeth and therefore never lyeth... Sir Phillip Sidney (1580)

NEW YORK MISCELLANY
pages.handwritten_text:
Felix Topolski 1956
Ben Sonnenberg at the "Pavilion"
sketch_descriptions: A loosely rendered sketch of a man with a mustache and bald head reclining in a chair.
A loosely sketched figure of a woman wearing a large hat on the left side of the page.
Two indistinct figures in the background appearing to be engaged in conversation, one wearing a hat.
pages.page_summary: This 1956 issue of Topolski's Chronicle features a pen sketch portraying Ben Sonnenberg at a place called 'Pavilion' along with a woman in a hat, framed under the theme 'New York Miscellany' and referencing historic Sir Phillip Sidney.

pages.handwritten_text:
Mr and Mrs Sidney Helmet
Mr Judith Truman Capote
Pent-House Party in Gracie Square
White Horse Tavern Greenwich Village
Metropolitan Opera Flluminion e Exhibition Spadina Chapel Ceiling
At El Morocco Salvadore Dali
Dia de Guadalupe in Styxet Church
SACRE COEUR DE JESUS 'ongies maed [?] chasce ? wood. It contains the largest heart shape in the world which lies front in Tahiti. The ? representing the blood of Christ are petit [?] urns placed in the bloodiest part ...
sketch_descriptions: A loose line drawing of four people sitting around a table, apparently at a pent-house party, labeled with the names Mr and Mrs Sidney Helmet, Mr Judith, and Truman Capote.
A sketch of people gathered and a scene from the Metropolitan Opera showing an exhibition or scene involving the Spadina Chapel ceiling, with a figure in uniform and others in dresses.
A drawing of a group of people seated together at the White Horse Tavern in Greenwich Village.
A smaller sketch showing a couple of figures, annotated as 'At El Morocco' with mention of Salvador Dali.
A sketch of a religious scene titled 'Dia de Guadalupe' in Styxet Church depicting a crucifix with accompanying text about Sacre Coeur de Jesus and related symbolic elements.
pages.page_summary: This page features multiple expressive sketches depicting social gatherings, notable individuals, and cultural or religious scenes, including a pent-house party, the White Horse Tavern, the Metropolitan Opera, and a religious artwork scene, with handwritten annotations on each.

pages.handwritten_text:
U.N. meeting on Hungary
Shapiro
SUBER Rodshy(?) U.N. Assembly(?)
sketch_descriptions: Top drawing shows a chaotic scene of U.N. delegates at a meeting, with labels visible for Hungary and Finland desks, dense figures and an overhead view of the assembly hall.
Bottom drawing depicts another angle of the U.N. Assembly with delegates from Egypt, France, India, Finland and Hungary, heavily sketched with emphasis on crowd and seating arrangements.

events: U.N.O. discussions on Hungary and Suez

pages.page_summary: The page features sketches and commentary on U.N. debates addressing the political situations in Hungary and Suez, capturing the intensity and crowded atmosphere of the assembly sessions.

pages.handwritten_text:
Engagement Ball at Gramercy Park
Alice Rubin Blier
A "Gourmet" Club banquet at the Waldorf Astoria
O'Neill's "The Iceman Cometh" at the Circle-in-the-Square Theatre, Greenwich Village
sketch_descriptions: Portrait of a woman identified as Alice Rubin Blier with seated and standing figures in the background, sketched at an engagement ball at Gramercy Park.
A banquet scene at the Waldorf Astoria featuring a man in a tuxedo standing or leaning over a table, surrounded by other guests.
A group of loosely sketched figures closely gathered, showing a variety of postures and expressions.
A dramatic scene from O'Neill's play 'The Iceman Cometh' at the Circle-in-the-Square Theatre, depicting actors in various seated and hunched positions around a table, conveying a somber mood.
pages.page_summary: The page features expressive pen sketches capturing social and theatrical scenes, including an engagement ball, a gourmet club banquet, and a theatrical performance of O'Neill's 'The Iceman Cometh' in New York venues.

pages.typeset_text:
THIS DOUBLE ISSUE COSTS 7s. ($1.00) "TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s. 6d. (50c) A COPY.
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £4. 4s. ($12 or equivalent); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA; AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME
Handprinted in Great Britain by Poets & Painters' Press 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1. from blocks made by Agusth. Miles Ltd and published from Bridge Arch 188, Saston Walk, London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved.
sketch_descriptions: Sketch of a crowded urban street scene during the New York Thanksgiving Day Parade featuring policemen, large parade balloons, and a crowd of spectators.
Sketch of the interior of a thrift shop labeled 'Council Thrift Shop', showing several figures looking at items including paintings and household objects.
pages.page_summary: The page features two expressive sketches depicting scenes from the New York Thanksgiving Day Parade and a council thrift shop, accompanied by publication and subscription details for Topolski's Chronicle.
